reasoning: 386 hanabi entries from Wikipedia/Wikidata authoritative list. Query is open 'I want to see fireworks'; response delivers the full national list. Most items lack prefecture/coordinates/dates, limiting direct trip use. No ranking or season filter applied but scope is correct.
missing_data: Dates/months of each festival; prefectures missing for many entries; no crowd or prominence ranking to help agent surface top events
improvement_hint: Add structured date fields (month, typical_date) and rank by prominence; filter top-N by default so agent gets actionable choices not raw 386-item dump

reasoning: Japanese-language query served with lang=ja correctly. Same 386-item Wikipedia hanabi list. Data is authoritative; constraint (language) fully satisfied. Weakness is the same as L3-01: no date, missing prefecture on many items, no ranking.
missing_data: Festival dates/months; prefecture coverage sparse; no prominence ranking
improvement_hint: Same as L3-01: add month/date fields and limit default output to top-N with sort by prominence_score

reasoning: Query asks for snow festivals; tool called get_festivals(prefecture=Hokkaido) but returned 0 festival items — Hokkaido snow festival data is absent. national_heritage section returns UNESCO/intangible performing arts (Dainichido Bugaku, Hayachine Kagura, etc.) unrelated to snow festivals. Data returned is authentic but completely off-topic for the intent.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival, Sounkyo Ice Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival — no snow-festival entries for Hokkaido at all
improvement_hint: Ingest Hokkaido yuki-matsuri entries (Sapporo Snow Festival is a major draw); also widen kind_tag to include 'yuki_matsuri' alongside existing hanabi entries

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks for rural life experience. Tool returned get_japan_heritage which contains 104 cultural heritage narratives. top_canonical_heritage surfaces Jo-an teahouse, Nagoya Castle, Sannai-Maruyama — primarily archaeological/architectural, not rural-stay experiences. RULE A applied: tool choice is wrong (should be search_area or get_spots with 農村 or 民泊), but the data returned is authoritative. Score 2 for intent, 3 for constraint (Chinese query, data in Japanese/English only — RULE D -1).
missing_data: Satoyama experience programs, rural guesthouses (minpaku), traditional farmhouse villages like Shirakawa-go as a stay destination
improvement_hint: A 'rural-stay' or 'satoyama' kind_tag plus integration of MLIT rural tourism programs would directly address this query class

reasoning: Korean-language query; response returns en/ja name fields with no Korean translation — RULE D applies, constraint_handling -1. Results do include sakura_meisho_100-tagged spots (Kenroku-en, Matsumae Castle, Himeji Castle) relevant to 紅葉 autumn-leaf viewing adjacent intent. However 紅葉 search pulls in entities with '紅葉' in name (Momijidani, etc.) rather than broader autumn-foliage destinations.
missing_data: Korean names/descriptions; major koyo spots like Nikko, Kyoto Tofuku-ji, Arashiyama not surfaced from this literal kanji search

reasoning: Query for quiet meditation place, not touristy. search_area(修行) surfaces Shugyoji Temple (Tokyo), Manshuin (Kyoto), Rinzaiji tokubetsu haikan + shugyō experience (Shizuoka DMO, ¥3,000 with bookable URL), Akame ninja experience. Rinzaiji result is highly practical with address, access, price. Multiple authentic sources. One notable caveat: result mix includes ninja experience which is not meditative, slightly off-intent.
missing_data: Zazen experience programs at less-visited temples (Eiheiji, Sojiji); accessibility of booking for non-Japanese speakers
improvement_hint: Tag zazen-experience venues separately from generic 修行 to surface quiet temple stays rather than ninja attractions

reasoning: Exemplary infeasibility response. not_available block gives accurate scientific explanation (auroras not viable from Japan), offers alt_kinds: dark_sky/observatory, surfaces alternatives including Sendai Astronomical Observatory, Kushiro Marsh, Akashi Planetarium. Spanish query served with en/ja in not_available text — RULE D: constraint_handling -1. But the core response is outstanding.
missing_data: Spanish-language version of the not_available explanation
improvement_hint: Localise not_available reason text into the requested lang (es/fr/de) so agent can relay it verbatim to the end user

reasoning: Query asks to sleep in a 100-year-old house (古民家). Tool returned get_hotels(Gifu) which surfaces onsen ryokan (hito_yu_kai) and generic OSM hotels/hostels. None are identified as kominka (old farmhouse). Gifu does have famous kominka areas (Shirakawa-go) but they aren't flagged. RULE A: wrong tool/filter but data is real; score 2 for intent.

improvement_hint: Add lodging_type='kominka' or heritage tag to hotels from heritage-designated districts; surface Shirakawa-go/Gokayama farmhouse accommodation

reasoning: Thai-language query for off-the-beaten-path spots; search_area(桜) returns 215 results with sakura_meisho_100 and famous spots (Kenroku-en, Himeji Castle). reasoning: Query asks to swim with wild dolphins. search_area(イルカ) returns 61 results but top hit is a Kyoto tourism ambassador page with no dolphin content. reasoning: Query for traditional architecture untouched by modernization — classic 重伝建 (Judenken) use case.
